title: Industrial Ecologists
description: Apply principles and processes of natural ecosystems to develop models for efficient industrial systems. Use knowledge from the physical and social sciences to maximize effective use of natural resources in the production and use of goods and services. Examine societal issues and their relationship with both technical systems and the environment.

tasks.text: Identify environmental impacts caused by products, systems, or projects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify or develop strategies or methods to minimize the environmental impact of industrial production processes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze changes designed to improve the environmental performance of complex systems and avoid unintended negative consequences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct environmental sustainability assessments, using material flow analysis (MFA) or substance flow analysis (SFA) techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Identify sustainable alternatives to industrial or waste-management practices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review research literature to maintain knowledge on topics related to industrial ecology, such as physical science, technology, economy, and public policy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Redesign linear, or open-loop, systems into cyclical, or closed-loop, systems so that waste products become inputs for new processes, modeling natural ecosystems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare technical and research reports, such as environmental impact reports, and communicate the results to individuals in industry, government, or the general public.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine local, regional, or global use and flow of materials or energy in industrial production processes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor the environmental impact of development activities, pollution, or land degradation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Build and maintain databases of information about energy alternatives, pollutants, natural environments, industrial processes, and other information related to ecological change.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform analyses to determine how human behavior can affect, and be affected by, changes in the environment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend methods to protect the environment or minimize environmental damage from industrial production practices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Translate the theories of industrial ecology into eco-industrial practices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop alternative energy investment scenarios to compare economic and environmental costs and benefits.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Carry out environmental assessments in accordance with applicable standards, regulations, or laws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Examine societal issues and their relationship with both technical systems and the environment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan or conduct field research on topics such as industrial production, industrial ecology, population ecology, and environmental production or sustainability.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create complex and dynamic mathematical models of population, community, or ecological systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate the effectiveness of industrial ecology programs, using statistical analysis and applications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Forecast future status or condition of ecosystems, based on changing industrial practices or environmental conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review industrial practices, such as the methods and materials used in construction or production, to identify potential liabilities and environmental hazards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Apply new or existing research about natural ecosystems to understand economic and industrial systems in the context of the environment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare plans to manage renewable resources.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify or compare the component parts or relationships between the parts of industrial, social, and natural systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan or conduct studies of the ecological implications of historic or projected changes in industrial processes or development.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research sources of pollution to determine environmental impact or to develop methods of pollution abatement or control.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform environmentally extended input-output (EE I-O) analyses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Promote use of environmental management systems (EMS) to reduce waste or to improve environmentally sound use of natural resources.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate the impact of changed land management or land use practices on ecosystems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or test protocols to monitor ecosystem components and ecological processes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research environmental effects of land and water use to determine methods of improving environmental conditions or increasing outputs, such as crop yields.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide industrial managers with technical materials on environmental issues, regulatory guidelines, or compliance actions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct applied research on the effects of industrial processes on the protection, restoration, inventory, monitoring, or reintroduction of species to the natural environment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct scientific protection, mitigation, or restoration projects to prevent resource damage, maintain the integrity of critical habitats, and minimize the impact of human activities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Investigate accidents affecting the environment to assess ecological impact.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct analyses to determine the maximum amount of work that can be accomplished for a given amount of energy in a system, such as industrial production systems and waste treatment systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate the adaptability of various animal and plant species to changed environmental conditions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
